title: Hindu Shahis
summary:
The Hindu Shahis, also referred to as the Kabul Shahis and Odi Shahis, were a dynasty that ruled Kabulistan, Gandhara and Punjab between 822 CE and 1026 CE, succeeding the Turk Shahis.
The dynasty was founded by Kallar in c. 822 CE after overthrowing Lagaturman, the last Turk Shahi king. His successor Samanta lost the region of Kabul to the Saffarid dynasty, but Lalliya soon re-conquered Kabul and subdued the region of Zabulistan as well. He also defeated Sankaravarman of the Utpala dynasty. Bhimadeva struggled against the Samanid dynasty, while the reign of his successor Jayapala saw conflict with the newly formed Ghaznavid dynasty. He was defeated and lost territories west of Indus in 1001 CE. The Shahis continue to resist the Ghaznavids for over two decades but were unsuccessful, resulting in the fall of the dynasty in 1026 CE.
text:
The Hindu Shahis, also referred to as the Kabul Shahis and Odi Shahis, were a dynasty that ruled Kabulistan, Gandhara and Punjab between 822 CE and 1026 CE, succeeding the Turk Shahis.
The dynasty was founded by Kallar in c. 822 CE after overthrowing Lagaturman, the last Turk Shahi king. His successor Samanta lost the region of Kabul to the Saffarid dynasty, but Lalliya soon re-conquered Kabul and subdued the region of Zabulistan as well. He also defeated Sankaravarman of the Utpala dynasty. Bhimadeva struggled against the Samanid dynasty, while the reign of his successor Jayapala saw conflict with the newly formed Ghaznavid dynasty. He was defeated and lost territories west of Indus in 1001 CE. The Shahis continue to resist the Ghaznavids for over two decades but were unsuccessful, resulting in the fall of the dynasty in 1026 CE.
